title: Outbound-Only Internet for Private Instances
description:
## Patching Without Exposure

Application servers run in **private subnets** of a VPC. They must be able to reach the internet **outbound** to download OS package updates and call a third-party HTTPS API, but they must **never be directly reachable from the internet inbound**. The instances use **IPv4** only.

Which solution satisfies both requirements?

options.text: Deploy a **NAT Gateway** in a public subnet and add a route in the private subnets' route table sending `0.0.0.0/0` to the NAT Gateway.
Attach an **Internet Gateway** route (`0.0.0.0/0 -> igw`) directly to the private subnets' route table so the instances can reach the internet.
Add an **egress-only Internet Gateway** to the private subnets and route `0.0.0.0/0` to it.
Move the application servers into the **public subnet** and rely on their security groups to block inbound traffic.

explanation: A **NAT Gateway** is purpose-built for exactly this: it lives in a *public* subnet, and private instances route their `0.0.0.0/0` traffic to it. The NAT Gateway initiates outbound connections on their behalf and allows the return traffic, but because connections can only be *initiated outbound*, the private instances remain unreachable from the internet — meeting both requirements. Option B is wrong: routing a private subnet directly to an **Internet Gateway** is what *makes a subnet public*; with public IPs the instances would become inbound-reachable. Option C is a trap — an **egress-only Internet Gateway** provides outbound-only access but only for **IPv6** traffic, and the workload is IPv4. Option D technically can work with tight security groups, but it violates the design principle of keeping app servers private and relies entirely on firewall rules being correct; the question explicitly wants the servers to stay in private subnets.

hints.title: Outbound-only IPv4 has a dedicated service
hints.body: For IPv4 'outbound but never inbound,' AWS gives you the NAT Gateway. It sits in a public subnet and the private subnets route to it.

hints.title: Watch the IPv6 decoy
hints.body: Egress-only Internet Gateway sounds perfect but it is IPv6-only. The scenario says IPv4.
